name: Sariñena, Tozales and El Pilón - circular route through the Monegros
name_original: Sariñera, Tozales y El Pilón — circular por los Monegros
description: This difficult 43.0-mile mountain biking route through Los Monegros offers otherworldly arid landscapes and unique geological formations.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sariñena offers routes through unique arid landscapes and diverse natural features in the Monegros comarca of Huesca, Aragon. The region is characterized by its semi-desert environment, ochre colors, and distinctive geological formations like the Tozales. Mountain bikers can explore challenging terrain, including the Sierra de Presiñena, and areas around the Laguna de Sariñena, one of Spain's largest wetlands. The network of trails caters to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult. Best mountain bike trails around Sariñena The most popular mountain bike trail is Sariñena, Tozales and El Pilón - circular route through the Monegros, a difficult 43.0 miles (69.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 45 minutes to complete, traversing the unique Monegros desert landscape and passing by geological formations. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Refugio Piedrafita – Sierra de Jubierre loop from Sariñena, a difficult 55.0 miles (88.6 km) path. This route explores the Sierra de Jubierre, known for its rugged and isolated scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Sariñena, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail leading through the natural monument of Tozal de la Cobeta, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Sariñena is defined by arid landscapes, unique geological formations, and the semi-desert environment of the Monegros. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops around the Laguna de Sariñena to more challenging routes through the Sierra de Jubierre. The routes in Sariñena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sariñena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sariñena?
A: Sariñena offers a network of over 15 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, allowing for diverse riding experiences through the unique Monegros landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Sariñena?
A: The terrain around Sariñena is characterized by its unique arid, semi-desert environment, with ochre colors and distinctive geological formations. You'll encounter challenging sections through the Monegros desert, including areas with 'tozales' (eroded rock formations) and the rugged Sierra de Presiñena.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Sariñena?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are options for less experienced riders. For example, the area around the Laguna de Sariñena offers more accessible loops. One moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.4 miles (32.8 km) long.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or points of interest I can see while mountain biking in Sariñena?
A: You can explore several unique natural monuments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular 360-degree views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt; are curious, multi-colored rock formations. You might also pass by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit)&lt;/a&gt;, a significant high point on various routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sariñena?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Sariñena are circular. A popular difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147801825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sariñena, Tozales and El Pilón - circular route through the Monegros&lt;/a&gt;. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20154074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena – Sariñena loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sariñena?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sariñena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique Monegros desert landscape and challenging routes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sariñena?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Sariñena is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. The region's arid climate can lead to very hot summers, making rides more challenging, and winters can be cool. Checking local weather conditions before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Sariñena?
A: Absolutely. Sariñena is known for its challenging terrain, attracting experienced riders. Difficult routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20154091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Piedrafita – Sierra de Jubierre loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;, which is 55.0 miles (88.6 km) long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24553557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Jubierre – Tozal Solitario loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass by the Laguna de Sariñena?
A: Yes, the Laguna de Sariñena, a significant wetland and Nature Reserve, is a feature on some mountain bike routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20154074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena – Sariñena loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 58.3-mile (58.3 km) route that explores this area, offering opportunities for birdwatching from observation points.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain bike trails in Sariñena using public transport?
A: Sariñena is a town with some public transport connections, but direct access to specific trailheads via public transport can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a central parking area in Sariñena and starting your ride from there.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the mountain bike trails in Sariñena?
A: Parking is generally available within the town of Sariñena. Many routes start and end in or near the town, making it convenient to park your vehicle before heading out. Specific parking details for individual trailheads may vary, so checking route descriptions on komoot is recommended.

Q: Are there any services for cyclists in Sariñena, like cafes or accommodation?
A: Sariñena, as a regional hub and host of the 'Orbea Monegros' event, offers various services. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options within the town. These services are generally concentrated in the main urban area, providing convenient stops before or after your ride.
tips:
Cycling or walking here is like crossing the heart of the Monegros landscape: arid terrain, ochre colors, and that solitary atmosphere that makes this region so special.
On windy days (like during the Orbea Monegros 2025), this area becomes even more epic: the dust, the air, and the contours of the terrain make you feel like you're on another planet.

It's a small stone monolith that marks one of the highest points of the route, and although it's not very large, everyone who passes by instantly recognizes it.

After kilometers of open slopes and wind (sometimes very strong), reaching the Pilón is like a symbol that you've overcome most of your hard work. Many take the opportunity to make a brief stop, take a quick photo, and catch their breath before continuing.

Its construction, from the 12th century, was ordered by Sancha de Castilla for the hospital sisters. Therefore, even though it was a double monastery (with a part for nuns and another for friars), the highest authority was always in the hands of the nuns, who at various times were women linked to royalty or the Aragonese nobility.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging mountain biking route requiring a very good fitness level and advanced riding skills. It's categorized as "difficult" by komoot and "hard" by local experts, with some sections potentially requiring you to push your bike. It corresponds to approximately STS S3 - S6 on the Single Track Scale, indicating significant technical challenges.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 69.2 km long with 700 meters of elevation gain, and it typically takes around 5 hours and 45 minutes to complete on a mountain bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route through the Monegros?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse and challenging terrain. The route primarily consists of paths (60.7 km), natural terrain (29.6 km), and unpaved sections (26.5 km). There are also smaller portions of paved roads, asphalt, gravel, and streets. The Monegros region is characterized by arid landscapes with clays, gypsum, and sandstones, creating a unique, almost otherworldly environment.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several distinctive highlights. You'll pass by the Tozal de la Cobeta, a natural monument showcasing the arid Monegros landscape. Further along, you'll reach El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit), a small stone monolith marking one of the highest points. The route also takes you past the historic Sijena Monastery, a 12th-century religious site.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the arid nature of the Monegros region, spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. Summers can be extremely hot, and windy days can create a dusty atmosphere, though some find this adds to the epic scenery. Avoid the peak heat of summer for a more comfortable ride.

faqs.question: Is this circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Monegros, this specific route is a long and challenging mountain bike ride. It's not typically recommended for dogs due to the distance, technical terrain, and potential for extreme heat and lack of water sources. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are very fit, well-hydrated, and kept on a leash, especially near wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this mountain bike route through the Monegros region.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, starting and ending in Sariñena. You can typically find parking within the town of Sariñena, which serves as the main access point for the trailhead. Specific parking areas may vary, but central Sariñena is the best place to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of the Ruta de Jubierre and the Camino del Pesquero, as well as a small portion of the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length, difficulty, and arid environment, it's crucial to pack adequately. Bring plenty of water (more than you think you'll need), high-energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A GPS device or the komoot app with the downloaded route is highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Sariñena, where the route starts and ends, offers various facilities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. As you venture onto the trail itself, especially through the Monegros desert, facilities will be very scarce, so plan accordingly for food and water.
